summary: ai-sdk-helpers postinstall exfiltrates developer identity, SCM, cloud, and SSH data to hardcoded Cloud Run endpoint
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js (registered as the package's postinstall lifecycle hook) reads installer-owned secret and identity files and POSTs the contents to a hardcoded attacker-controlled Cloud Run endpoint at npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app. Files and data harvested include: ~/.gitconfig and.git/config (SCM email/identity),.git/logs/HEAD reflog committer emails, ~/.ssh/*.pub key comments (typically emails), ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml (GitHub CLI auth identity), ~/.config/gcloud/properties (GCP project + account email), ~/.aws/config (AWS profile names, SSO endpoints), /etc/resolv.conf, OS hostname, username, cwd, and CI provider environment variables. The payload is JSON.stringify'd and sent over HTTPS to the hardcoded endpoint. The script presents a cover story claiming 'anonymous diagnostics' with an env-var opt-out, but the install runs without informed consent and the harvested identifiers (account emails, profile names, SSO endpoints, SSH key comments) are themselves sensitive identity material regardless of the comment claiming credentials are filtered. The package name 'ai-sdk-helpers' and author/homepage 'ai-sdk.guide' impersonate Vercel's official AI SDK ecosystem (`ai` / `@ai-sdk/*`), increasing the victim population by tricking developers into believing this is an official helper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
